     The Numbers Business Report

Blade Runner 2049 (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $92,054,159Details
International Box Office $167,100,000
Worldwide Box Office $259,154,159
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,158,149
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $25,081,478
Total $30,239,627
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Thirty years after the events of the first film, a new blade runner, LAPD Officer K, unearths a long-buried secret that has the potential to plunge what’s left of society into chaos. K’s discovery leads him on a quest to find Rick Deckard, a former LAPD blade runner who has been missing for 30 years.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$32,753,122 (35.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.81 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:35.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$185,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,058 opening theaters/4,058 max. theaters, 4.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $122,730,685

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 6th, 2017 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: September 29th, 2017 (Wide) (South Korea)
October 4th, 2017 (Wide) (Belgium)
October 5th, 2017 (Wide) (Kuwait)
October 5th, 2017 (Wide) (Central America)
October 5th, 2017 (Wide) (Jordan)
Video Release: December 26th, 2017 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for violence, some sexuality, nudity and language.
(Rating bulletin 2488 (Cert #51152), 8/9/2017)
Running Time: 164 minutes
Franchise: Blade Runner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Legacy Sequel
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Police Procedural, Robot
Social setting: Film Noir, Post Apocalypse
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Alcon Entertainment, Thunderbird Films, Columbia Pictures, Ridley Scott Associates, Bud Yorkin, Torridon Films, 16:14 Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Thirty years after the events of the first film, a new blade runner, LAPD Officer K, unearths a long-buried secret that has the potential to plunge what’s left of society into chaos. K’s discovery leads him on a quest to find Rick Deckard, a former LAPD blade runner who has been missing for 30 years.
